message.content:
@startuml
skinparam backgroundColor #FFFFFF
skinparam sequenceMessageAlign center
skinparam participantStyle rectangle
skinparam noteBackgroundColor #FFFFE0
skinparam noteBorderColor #000000
skinparam noteFontColor #000000
skinparam participantBackgroundColor #E6E6FA
skinparam participantBorderColor #000000
skinparam participantFontColor #000000
skinparam arrowColor #000000
skinparam arrowThickness 1
skinparam lifelineColor #000000
skinparam lifelineThickness 1

box "sw-explorer" #D3D3D3
    participant WebApp
    participant SWData
end box

box "Example background processing module" #D3D3D3
    participant Manager
    participant MessageQueue
    participant Worker
    participant Memcache
end box

WebApp -> WebApp: fetch new collection
WebApp -> SWData: fetch new collection
SWData -> Manager: schedule a job

note over Manager, Memcache
This is just an example.
We could got wi go with Celery, but sometimes it's good
to hide the concrete engine behind the abstraction layer.
end note

Manager -> MessageQueue: schedule a job
MessageQueue -> Worker: execute a job
Worker -> Manager: update job status
Manager -> Memcache: update job status
Worker -> Manager: job result
Manager -> Memcache: job result

WebApp -> WebApp: view pending collection
WebApp -> SWData: get job status
SWData -> Manager: get job status
Manager -> Memcache: get job status

@enduml
